paper.title_en: Quantum-enhanced collective emission in twisted noncommutative gauge theories
paper.abstract_en: We develop a theoretical framework to analyze collective quantum emission phenomena in the presence of twisted noncommutative (NC) gauge structures and curved spacetime backgrounds. Starting from a self-consistent formulation of NC gauge theories based on deformation quantization and twisted symmetries, we derive a generalized dynamical description of interacting atomic arrays and their radiative properties within a field-theoretic setting. In this case, we demonstrate that twisted noncommutativity generates nontrivial modifications to collective spontaneous emission, producing corrections in decay rates, coherence properties, and radiation patterns through deformation-dependent interaction terms. Also, the analysis further establishes that these effects strongly modify quantum-enhanced sensitivity, where deviations from standard scaling laws arise due to the interplay between NC parameters and many-body correlations, allowing transitions beyond the standard quantum limit toward the Heisenberg limit. By incorporating gravitational effects, we obtain covariant corrections to collective emission processes in curved spacetimes, showing a coupling between spacetime geometry and NC deformation that modifies the underlying quantum dynamics. A fully covariant formulation in arbitrary D dimensions is developed, ensuring consistency with relativistic principles and gauge invariance. In this context, the obtained results show a unified description of quantum collective phenomena under simultaneous geometric and NC effects, with direct implications for precision measurements, quantum technologies, and fundamental tests of quantum field theory in nontrivial backgrounds.
